discussion_digest: The biggest story today is Intel (INTC), which saw a massive surge in Reddit mentions from 18 to 298 in 24 hours, jumping from rank 30 to 6. The community is buzzing about its strongest revenue growth in 15 years and raised guidance, with many calling it a turnaround. However, skeptics argue that chip stocks have topped out and that even blowout earnings can't push prices higher, as seen with MU. The bullish sentiment on Stocktwits (12 vs 8 bearish) suggests retail is leaning positive, but the debate is heated.

Tesla (TSLA) reclaimed the top spot on Reddit with 416 mentions, up from 247, and a bullish sentiment on Stocktwits (20 vs 7 bearish). The hype seems tied to SpaceX (SPCX) chatter, as many posts mention both tickers. Meanwhile, software stocks like ORCL and NOW are gaining traction after ORCL's $7 billion Pentagon contract, with extremely bullish sentiment (23 vs 3 and 21 vs 3 respectively). The community sees this as a catalyst for the software sector, with users expecting gap-ups and short squeezes.

The crowd is split on the market's direction. While many are bullish on tech earnings and AI, others point to macro risks like tariffs and rising oil prices (USO mentions jumped from 18 to 74). The most-liked posts often combine bullish tickers with warnings about tariffs and yields, reflecting a cautious optimism. The tension is highest on SPCX, where bullish squeeze talk clashes with bearish delay warnings. This disagreement suggests that while retail is excited, they are also wary of external shocks.

overlooked_insights.title: SpaceX abort at engine ignition
overlooked_insights.insight: SpaceX's Starship Flight 13 attempt was scrubbed at T-0 due to an automated flight computer hold/abort call right as engine ignition started, not simply due to weather as headlines suggest. This hardware/software issue may indicate deeper problems with the launch system.
overlooked_insights.evidence: User message: 'SpaceX just scrubbed today's Starship Flight 13 attempt again. At T-0, the automated flight computer triggered a hold/abort call right as engine ignition started...'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream headlines summarized the delay as 'weather-related' while the community reported a specific technical abort during ignition.

overlooked_insights.title: Google holds $94B SpaceX stake
overlooked_insights.insight: A user claims Google disclosed a $94.1 billion investment in SpaceX, representing a 6% stake. This valuation is far higher than the $29 billion loss headlines reference, suggesting Alphabet's hidden asset value may be underestimated.
overlooked_insights.evidence: User message: 'GOOG Google discloses $94.1 billion in SpaceX, marking 6% stake.'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Headlines focus on the market cap loss from the tech selloff, not the detailed breakdown of Alphabet's SpaceX holdings.

overlooked_insights.title: ServiceNow AI Kill Switch Act
overlooked_insights.insight: Congress introduced the 'AI Kill Switch Act' following the OpenAI incident, and a user notes that ServiceNow's existing AI kill switch technology was repeatedly mentioned in the bill hearings. This legislation could become a major catalyst for NOW if it mandates such controls.
overlooked_insights.evidence: Multiple users: 'Congress introduced the Ai Kill Switch Act today... Bill kept bringing up Servicenow's Ai Kill Switch' and 'guess who has been positioning themselves as the AI control tower...'

Investors should know that the main debate is whether the CapEx increase is a smart investment or a risk, and that sentiment is heavily influenced by short-term market noise.
